items.title: Hardwick lashes 'peanut' football fans after fiery run-in with spectator
items.summary: Damien Hardwick clashes with Crows fans at Adelaide Oval, as Jeremy Cameron's injury timeline is revealed.

items.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
items.summary: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

items.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
items.summary: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

items.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
items.summary: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

items.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
items.summary: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

items.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
items.summary: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
items.summary: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

items.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
items.summary: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

items.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
items.summary: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

items.title: NATO was a lesson in how the geopolitical landscape has shifted around Trump
items.summary: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

items.title: BYD backflips and offers full refunds after customers given cars from wrong year
items.summary: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

items.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
items.summary: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

items.summary: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

items.title: Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer, tributes paid to 'incredible musician'
items.summary: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up new art gallery in Bangkok, Thailand
items.summary: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

items.title: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell in collision involving pedestrian
items.summary: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

items.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket

items.summary: Craig McRae takes aim at the AFL's ARC after Collingwood's thrilling win over North Melbourne.

items.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
items.summary: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

items.title: Six-year-old boy dies after two-vehicle crash at Gleneagle in the Scenic Rim
items.summary: A six-year-old boy has died in hospital following a two-vehicle crash near Beaudesert on Monday.

items.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
items.summary: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

items.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say
items.summary: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

items.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
items.summary: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

items.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
items.summary: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

items.title: Roundabout to be built at Bruce Highway–Walker St intersection in Maryborough
items.summary: The Queensland government will build a four-lane roundabout at a Bruce Highway intersection that claimed three lives.

items.title: Judge hands down verdict in Jayson Gillham and Melbourne Symphony Orchestra trial
items.summary: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

items.title: Wimbledon 2026: Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up all-Czech final as Coco Gauff misses golden chance
items.summary: Coco Gauff is left to rue a regulation miss on match point as Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up an all-Czech date at Wimbledon.

items.title: ASX closes the week higher after Telstra told to 'face the music' over outage — As it happened
items.summary: The Australian share market closed higher on Friday after the latest escalation of tensions in the Middle East sent oil prices higher. Telstra faced further pressure over the national network outage.

items.title: Hanson doubles down on call for Coalition to work with One Nation — as it happened
items.summary: Pauline Hanson has doubled down on calls for the Coalition to work with One Nation.

items.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war'
items.summary: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

items.title: Australian War Memorial makes 'magnificent' discovery of first Indigenous Military Cross recipient
items.summary: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

items.title: Aussie Rules club Fregon Bulldogs' new guernseys bring change in fortunes
items.summary: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

items.title: The joy of Neil: How a cheeky seal on Tasmania’s shores brought happiness to the world
items.summary: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

items.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate
items.summary: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

items.title: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific
items.summary: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

items.title: How Beijing 'tripled the size of its nuclear arsenal' in six years
items.summary: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

items.title: Narendra Modi given rapturous welcome as he addresses thousands at community event in Melbourne
items.summary: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

items.title: Three NT corrections officers stood down with pay over prisoner escape

items.title: Angus Taylor says One Nation policies would bring 'eternity of pain' to economy
items.summary: In his strongest remarks pushing back on One Nation, the opposition leader has accused the right-wing party of being a "column of smoke".

items.title: State of Origin Game III the highest-rating program in series history
items.summary: An unprecedented number of viewers tuned into the State of Origin decider, in which NSW stunned Queensland to win the series.

items.title: NT Police charge 34yo Jordan Garrawurra with murder after 17yo girl found dead in Galiwin'ku home
items.summary: Days after a 17-year-old girl was found dead inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, police have charged a 34-year-old man following an autopsy.

items.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
items.summary: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

items.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
items.summary: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

items.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run and slams IOC decision on Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

items.title: SA police investigating possible Telstra outage death — as it happened
items.summary: South Australian police confirm they are investigating a death possibly linked to Telstra outage. Look back at the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

items.summary: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has backed Sarah Henderson's decision to test-call Triple Zero, saying she was "doing her job".

items.summary: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

items.title: Birds in Melbourne's west have 'highly elevated' PFAS in their blood, study finds
items.summary: Blood samples found levels of the "forever chemicals" in sparrows near contaminated industrial and military sites were 10 times higher than those in a comparison population.

items.title: Report shows ambulance call-outs plummet during initial years of Richmond's medically supervised injecting room
items.summary: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

items.title: Australia issued blunt warning over AUKUS and China's nuclear capabilities
items.summary: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

items.title: Australian woman arrested in US after allegedly voting illegally in federal elections

items.title: Is AI changing your life in unexpected ways? Should we move faster or more cautiously? The ABC wants to hear from you

items.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
items.summary: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

items.title: China missile launch tests Pacific relations as Australia looks to pounce
items.summary: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

items.title: Cancer survivor Torstein Træen claims Tour de France yellow jersey on desperately hot stage four
items.summary: Torstein Træen was diagnosed with testicular cancer in 2022, leading to the removal of his left testicle. Three years later, he is the overall race leader at the Tour de France.

items.title: PBS dispute could leave MS patients with huge bills for crucial drugs Ocrevus and Kesimpta
items.summary: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.
